questions.question: A new plant species named Osbeckia zubeengargiana was recently discovered from which state?
questions.explanation: Researchers from Gauhati University discovered a new plant species Osbeckia zubeengargiana. It was found in the grasslands of Manas National Park, Assam. The species is named after famous Assamese singer Zubeen Garg, a rare botanical tribute to a cultural icon. The plant belongs to the Melastomataceae, known for diverse tropical and subtropical shrubs. It is a perennial erect shrub adapted to grassland ecosystems. The plant has delicate purple to pink four-petalled (tetramerous) flowers. It grows well in moist soil conditions, increasing floral diversity in grasslands. It usually flowers and bears fruit from mid-September to January.

questions.question: Megamalai Wildlife Sanctuary is located in which state?
questions.explanation: Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) discovered nine new species during a faunal survey in Megamalai Wildlife Sanctuary in Tamil Nadu. It is located in Tamil Nadu. It is part of the Western Ghats, a global biodiversity hotspot with rich wildlife diversity. The area supports many plants and animals, from shrubs and trees to mammals, birds, and insects.

questions.question: What are Petroglyphs that were recently mentioned in news?

options.label: A
options.text: Images engraved or carved on rock surfaces

answer.label: A
answer.text: Images engraved or carved on rock surfaces

questions.explanation: Researchers discovered two ancient petroglyphs near Beerappa Temple in Manchirevula, Telangana, inside a rock shelter. Petroglyphs are rock engravings made by removing surface material using techniques like pecking, incising, drilling, and polishing. They differ from petrographs, which are painted or drawn images on rocks. The term comes from Greek words “petros” (stone) and “glyphein” (to carve). Petroglyphs appear as scratches or deep carvings and are linked to prehistoric human activity. They are found worldwide except Antarctica, with major presence in Africa, Americas, Europe, Siberia, and Australia. In India, Edakkal Caves in Kerala is a famous example.

questions.question: The Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens (IPSrC) is a component of which scheme?
questions.explanation: The Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment implements the Integrated Programme for Senior Citizens (IPSrC) under the umbrella scheme Atal Vayo Abhyuday Yojana (AVYAY) for the welfare of elderly people. Under IPSrC, the government provides grant-in-aid to eligible NGOs and voluntary organisations for running projects such as Senior Citizen Homes, Continuous Care Homes, Mobile Medicare Units, Physiotherapy Units, and Regional Resource and Training Centres (RRTCs). HelpAge India has been designated as one of the Regional Resource and Training Centres (RRTCs) under the scheme.

questions.question: RELIEF – Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation intervention falls under which government initiative?
questions.explanation: The Government of India has approved RELIEF – Resilience & Logistics Intervention for Export Facilitation under the Export Promotion Mission (EPM) to support exporters affected by disruptions in the Gulf and wider West Asia maritime corridor. The intervention was prompted by heightened security concerns around the Strait of Hormuz, which led to vessel diversions, longer routes, congestion at transshipment hubs, and emergency conflict-linked surcharges, increasing logistics costs and operational uncertainty for exporters.

questions.question: Where was Exercise Amogh Jwala conducted by Indian army?
questions.explanation: The Indian Army’s Southern Command conducted Exercise Amogh Jwala at the Babina Field Firing Ranges, Uttar Pradesh, to demonstrate high-tempo mechanised warfare capabilities. The exercise showcased the integrated use of advanced military assets, including attack helicopters, fighter aircraft, drones, counter-drone systems, and modern battlefield technologies. It was conducted in a multi-domain operational environment, combining land, air, cyber, and space elements to reflect modern warfare requirements. The drill emphasized synchronisation of fire and manoeuvre, along with real-time drone-enabled surveillance and target acquisition.

questions.question: Legionnaires’ disease, that was recently seen in news, is caused by which agent?
questions.explanation: Recent Legionnaires’ disease cases in London and New York have raised global concern. Public health experts warn that densely populated cities, including those in India, could face similar outbreaks if preventive measures are not strengthened. Legionnaires’ disease (LD) is a severe pneumonia caused by the bacterium Legionella, first identified in 1976 at an American Legion convention. Transmission occurs when people breathe in water or soil containing Legionella; it is not contagious and generally cannot spread through drinking water unless aspirated. High-risk groups include older adults, smokers, and people with weakened immune systems.

questions.question: According to recent data, which state has become India’s top textile-exporting state?
questions.explanation: Tamil Nadu has become India’s top textile-exporting state, with shipments worth USD 7,997.17 million in 2024-25, a 29.12% increase over the last four years. In 2020-21, Tamil Nadu’s textile exports were USD 6,193 million, showing consistent growth under the Dravidian Model government, led by Chief Minister M. K. Stalin. The state now contributes 21.84% of India’s total textile exports, outpacing Gujarat (USD 5,646.01 million) and Maharashtra (USD 3,831.29 million). Data is reported through the National Import-Export Record for Yearly Analysis of Trade, providing real-time comprehensive foreign trade data.

questions.question: Which state/UT launched a mobile app called ‘Blue Voice’ to enhance safety and disaster preparedness for coastal fishing communities?
questions.explanation: Pondicherry launched a mobile app called ‘Blue Voice’ to enhance safety and disaster preparedness for coastal fishing communities. The app provides real-time, location-specific weather updates in a user-friendly format, addressing language barriers and complex interfaces of conventional platforms. It allows fishers to share experiential knowledge alongside modern weather alerts, creating a participatory digital platform for better decision-making at sea.

questions.question: Sarhul festival is mainly celebrated in which state?
questions.explanation: Sarhul is a major Adivasi festival centred on nature worship, symbolising the union of the Sun and Earth and the renewal of life. The festival involves worship of the Sal tree, believed to be the abode of Sama Maa, and is celebrated by tribes like Oraon, Munda, Santal, Khadia and Ho. It is mainly observed in Jharkhand and parts of Odisha, West Bengal and Chhattisgarh. It is a three-day festival ending with a community feast, after which agricultural activities like sowing and ploughing begin, highlighting its link with livelihood and nature.
